field_definitions.access_date: Date the official source was checked, in ISO 8601 format.
field_definitions.category: Registry grouping only; it does not rank legal or evidentiary authority.
field_definitions.conflict_notes: Known authority, jurisdiction, setting, or cross-source reconciliation cautions.
field_definitions.domains: Clinical, operational, coding, or interoperability areas for discovery.
field_definitions.effective_date: Included only when the effective date was verified.
field_definitions.id: Stable local identifier; not an identifier assigned by the source organisation.
field_definitions.jurisdiction: Geographic or program jurisdiction explicitly limiting use.
field_definitions.last_updated: Included only when an official update date was verified.
field_definitions.mapping_role: Permitted registry role; discovery roles require selection and verification of a specific child source before mapping.
field_definitions.publication_date: Included only when verified; may use YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-DD precision.
field_definitions.recency_notes: How to determine whether the source remains current.
field_definitions.reuse_notes: Conservative permissions and licensing handling; metadata inclusion does not grant content-reuse rights.
field_definitions.scope: What the source can support in workflow research.
field_definitions.source_type: Nature of the official source, such as regulation, payer manual, code-set portal, standard, guideline, or catalog.
field_definitions.superseded_notes: Replacement, retirement, version-selection, or historical-use cautions.
field_definitions.title: Exact visible document or landing-page title verified from the official source.
field_definitions.url: Exact official URL checked during live research.
field_definitions.version: Included only when the source displayed or published a verified version or edition.

registry_metadata.evidence_boundary: The registry identifies authoritative starting points. It does not assert that every source creates a documentation requirement or that the source set validates every workflow.
registry_metadata.exclusion_rule: Aggregators, commercial summaries, tertiary explainers, unverified URLs, uncertain titles, drafts presented as current, and sources with unresolved provenance were omitted.
registry_metadata.inclusion_rule: Only official primary issuers, government sources, standards or terminology owners, guideline developers, accreditors, and specialty societies verified by live research were included.
registry_metadata.intended_use: Conservative source discovery and provenance control for mapping approximately 1,500 clinical documentation workflows.
registry_metadata.mandatory_mapping_rule: For every workflow, verify the exact child document, section, version, effective date, jurisdiction, setting, population, payer, active status, and reuse permission before deriving a requirement.
registry_metadata.primary_operating_context: United States, with clearly labeled international sources retained only for jurisdiction-appropriate use or evidence comparison.
registry_metadata.title: Authoritative Source Registry for Cross-Specialty Clinical Documentation Workflow Mapping
registry_metadata.workflow_coverage_status: Not complete. Workflow-specific source gaps remain gaps and must not be filled by inference, analogy, or fabricated citations.
